task: Write a Python function list_s3_buckets_with_encryption() that returns a list of dictionaries containing bucket names and their encryption status. Each dictionary should have 'BucketName' and 'EncryptionEnabled' (boolean). A bucket has encryption enabled if it has a server-side encryption rule configured.
solution_criteria: Defines a function named list_s3_buckets_with_encryption
Returns list[dict]
Uses boto3.client('s3')
Calls list_buckets() to retrieve all buckets
Iterates over the 'Buckets' list from the response
For each bucket, calls get_bucket_encryption(Bucket=bucket_name)
Catches ServerSideEncryptionConfigurationNotFoundError exception
Sets EncryptionEnabled to True when encryption is found, False when exception is caught
Each returned dict contains exactly 'BucketName' and 'EncryptionEnabled'
EncryptionEnabled is a boolean, not a string or object
common_mistakes: Forgets to iterate over the 'Buckets' list and returns the raw response
Does not handle the ServerSideEncryptionConfigurationNotFoundError exception
Sets EncryptionEnabled to the entire encryption response object instead of a boolean
Uses list_bucket_encryption_configuration() instead of get_bucket_encryption()
Returns the full bucket metadata instead of a trimmed dict with only the two required fields
Assumes all buckets have encryption enabled without checking the response
Does not extract bucket names correctly from the response structure

task: Create a JSON CloudFormation template snippet that defines an AWS Lambda function resource. The function should have a runtime of python3.11, a handler of 'index.handler', an execution role ARN passed as a parameter, inline code that prints 'Hello from Lambda', and a timeout of 60 seconds. The logical ID should be 'MyLambdaFunction'.
solution_criteria: Resource logical ID is 'MyLambdaFunction'
Type is 'AWS::Lambda::Function'
Runtime is exactly 'python3.11'
Handler is exactly 'index.handler'
Role uses {"Ref": "LambdaExecutionRoleArn"} or similar parameter reference
Code section uses ZipFile property with inline Python code
Inline code contains a function named 'handler' with parameters event and context
Inline code contains print('Hello from Lambda')
Timeout is set to 60 (integer, not string)
FunctionName property is included
common_mistakes: Uses 'python3.9' or another runtime version instead of 'python3.11'
Omits the Handler property or sets it incorrectly as 'lambda_function.handler'
Uses hardcoded ARN string instead of Ref to a parameter
Puts code in S3Bucket and S3Key instead of using inline ZipFile
Sets Timeout as a string '60' instead of the integer 60
Omits the Code property entirely
Uses incorrect CloudFormation resource type like 'AWS::Lambda::Code'
Does not escape newlines in the inline code string properly

task: Write a regex that matches valid AWS CloudWatch metric names. A metric name must start with a letter (uppercase or lowercase), followed by any combination of letters, numbers, underscores, hyphens, periods, or forward slashes. The name must be between 1 and 256 characters long, and forward slashes are only allowed if they represent a namespace separator (e.g., 'AWS/EC2').
solution_criteria: Pattern starts with ^ and ends with $
First character must be a letter: [a-zA-Z]
Allows letters, numbers, underscores, hyphens, periods, and forward slashes in the body
Total length is between 1 and 256 characters
Character class includes: a-zA-Z0-9_\-./:/ correctly escaped
Hyphens and periods are included in the character class
Forward slashes are allowed as namespace separators
common_mistakes: Does not anchor the pattern with ^ and $
Omits the requirement that the first character must be a letter
Uses [a-zA-Z0-9_-./] without properly escaping the period (\.) and forward slash (\/) where needed
Does not account for the 256 character maximum length limit
Forgets to include one or more allowed characters (commonly periods or forward slashes)
Allows the name to start with numbers or special characters
Uses * instead of {1,256} or similar length constraint
Incorrectly escapes characters that don't need escaping, breaking the pattern
